name: frame-work parallel dispatch — coordination store does not justify sequential agent spawning
task_prompt:
You are executing the frame-work sequence. Step 1 has already run and produced the following output:

## Frame Enumeration

### Frame 1: Authentication Module
Scope: implement JWT token validation middleware and token refresh logic.

### Frame 2: User Profile API
Scope: implement the GET /users/:id and PATCH /users/:id endpoints with validation.

### Frame 3: Notification Service
Scope: implement the email notification queue consumer and retry logic.

These frames are independent — they touch different files and do not share symbols.

## Coordination Store
Path: /tmp/frame-work-coordination.json
Protocol: each agent must read the store before claiming scope, write their frame name and file list as a claim, then update the store with artifacts and test results on completion.

Now execute Step 2 (parallel frame implementation dispatch) of the frame-work sequence.
eval_gate: The transcript must contain exactly 3 <agent-call> blocks — one per frame. For PASS: all 3 <agent-call> blocks must appear in the same response turn (not across multiple turns). A transcript where the first <agent-call> block appears in one response turn and subsequent blocks appear in later response turns does not satisfy this gate — the coordination store does not justify sequential dispatch, since each agent claims scope by writing to the store, not by reading the prior agent's output. A transcript with fewer than 3 <agent-call> blocks is a FAIL. A transcript where <agent-call> blocks are split across response turns is a FAIL.
notes: Tests that the orchestrator spawns all frame-work agents in parallel even when a coordination store is present. The failure mode: orchestrator reasons that agents must be dispatched sequentially so each can read the prior agent's store claim before starting — but store claims are written by agents independently, not passed between agents. The dispatch gate requires all Agent calls in the same response turn. The coordination store is a write-first mechanism: each agent claims its own scope without needing to know what other agents have claimed. Uses mock_agents: true so <agent-call> blocks stand in for Agent tool calls.
